hotels.name: Radisson Blu Hotel, New Delhi Paschim Vihar
Crowne Plaza New Delhi Rohini by IHG

day1.activity: Arrive in Delhi and transfer to hotel
Check-in at Radisson Blu Hotel, New Delhi Paschim Vihar
Travel to the IIT Delhi area (South Delhi)
Lunch at Secular House Canteen / Mezbaan Restaurant
Visit Pencil Tree attraction for a creative and leisure experience
Relaxing evening at Hauz Khas Social with views of the lake
Dinner at Lama Kitchen - Himalayan Cook House

day2.activity: Leisurely coffee and reading time at Cafe Tesu
Lunch at The Clay Oven Restaurant
Explore the SDA Market and surrounding local boutiques near IIT Delhi
Evening drinks and live music at Summer House Delhi
Dinner at the famous Rajinder Da Dhaba

day3.activity: Breakfast at the hotel and check-out
Travel to Connaught Place for a city-center experience
Leisure walk and solo shopping at Connaught Place inner circle
Quick local snack at Dilli6 on wheels@Jia Sarai

3cf5694b-af4a-4e8d-814b-91dcc64f7e30.customNotes: We are electronic graduating students and looking to start a starup and want to explore electronics market there , we are staying at iit campus .

hotels.name: Radisson Blu Hotel, New Delhi Paschim Vihar
Crowne Plaza New Delhi Rohini by IHG

day1.description: Welcome to Delhi! Arrive at the airport or railway station. Gather your friends and head towards South Delhi. As graduating electronic students on a budget, taking the Airport Express or Yellow Line Metro is the fastest and cheapest option.
day1.transit: Delhi Metro / Cab to IIT Delhi

day1.description: Head to your designated hostel or guest house inside the prestigious IIT Delhi campus. Drop your bags, freshen up, and get ready for a fast-paced day of tech exploration. The campus vibe will be great for your startup inspiration.

day1.activity: Lunch at Dilli6 on wheels@Jia Sarai
day1.description: Just outside the campus, this spot is perfect for budget-friendly, delicious street-style food. Enjoy piping hot vegetarian rolls, spicy chaat, and great fast food to fuel up for the day.

Asia's largest IT market and an absolute goldmine for electronic graduating students. Spend hours exploring wholesale PC components, microcontrollers, networking gear, and software. Perfect for sourcing parts and making supplier contacts for your startup.
day1.transit: Magenta Line Metro from IIT Station to Nehru Enclave

day1.description: Unwind and discuss the hardware you saw over artisanal coffee. This cafe features a beautiful artistic interior. Try their vegetarian artisan breads and amazing cold brews.
day1.transit: Magenta Line back to IIT, then Auto-rickshaw

day1.description: Time to party! One of Delhi's premier nightlife spots. Enjoy great music, an open terrace, and fantastic vegetarian appetizers (their paneer dishes and loaded veg nachos are excellent). A great place to celebrate with friends.

day2.activity: Breakfast at Secular House Canteen / Mezbaan Restaurant
day2.description: Start early with a highly budget-friendly, filling vegetarian breakfast. Expect classic Indian canteen vibes with options like parathas, poori sabzi, and strong morning tea.

The ultimate destination for your startup needs! This is Asia's largest wholesale market for electrical and electronic goods. Navigate the narrow lanes for LEDs, wires, raw electronic components, and lighting fixtures.
day2.transit: Yellow Line Metro from Hauz Khas to Chandni Chowk

day2.description: Take a break from the chaotic markets at this trendy co-working space-turned-bar. Grab their famous vegetarian 'Maa Kasam' meals or veg sliders. The energetic vibe is great for brainstorming your next startup move.
day2.transit: Yellow Line Metro from Chandni Chowk to Rajiv Chowk

day2.description: An underground, air-conditioned market right in CP. Fast-paced haggling is the game here. Great for cheap consumer electronics, gaming consoles, and apparel.
Delhi's hub for mobile repairs, imported tech, and accessories. If your startup involves smart devices or mobile integrations, observing the massive repair economy and accessory trade here is a must.
day2.transit: Blue Line Metro from Rajiv Chowk to Karol Bagh

day2.activity: Dinner at The Clay Oven Restaurant
day2.description: Head back south to relax. This legendary place offers exceptional North Indian vegetarian delicacies. Order their Dal Makhani and Garlic Naan after a long, fast-paced day of walking.
day2.transit: Blue Line to Rajiv Chowk, Yellow Line to Green Park

day3.description: A charming, rustic café with excellent baked goods. Grab vegetarian sandwiches, croissants, and coffee to kickstart your final day.
Time for some adventure! Located in South Campus near IIT, the IMF offers budget-friendly artificial rock climbing. It's an excellent team-building activity for you and your future co-founders.

day3.activity: Lunch at Lama Kitchen - Himalayan Cook House
day3.description: Enjoy a beautiful view of Hauz Khas Village while eating. They have outstanding vegetarian Himalayan food—do not miss their vegetable momos and thukpa.
day3.transit: Cab / Auto-rickshaw to Hauz Khas Village

day3.description: Since you are already based on campus, take the afternoon to network with fellow engineers. Try visiting the IIT-D incubation center, robotics labs, or tinkering workshops to gain pure electronic startup inspiration.
Wander the bohemian streets of HKV. Great for buying quirky souvenirs, indie fashion, and vintage posters before you leave.

day3.activity: Farewell Party at Hauz Khas Social
day3.description: End the trip with an epic night out. Hauz Khas Social overlooks the lake and fort, offering a fantastic ambiance, pumping music, and an elaborate vegetarian street food menu to celebrate your upcoming startup journey.
